other_observers: No one else saw the bird while it was alive;
specific_location: Small lake next to the Doubletree hotel at 1775 E Cheyenne Mountain Blvd, right across from Target
description: Gull that was swimming near a shore in a lake. Small yellow bill with no marks on it. Dark eyes. Back of head, nape, and mantle were a uniform shade of gray. All coverts were grey. Front half of crown and malar were white, prominent vertical dark streak behind eye. White tail, tips of primaries were black. White belly and breast. This gull was considerably smaller than the common ring-beaked gulls that I have seen.Behaviors: This bird was swimming in a small area near shore and seemed disoriented. It did not swim away when I approached it.Call: None heardPlumage: adult
